channel.title: AI & Org, Curated
channel.description: A curated weekly digest on AI and organizational design — where invention meets innovation meets real impact.

item.title: Issue #7: AI's Report Card Is In, Opus 4.7 Has Mixed Reviews, and What the First Emperor Can Teach You About Standardization
item.description: Stanford AI Index drops (50-84% of K-12 students use AI for schoolwork), JP Morgan's AI economics deck, Opus 4.7 ships with hallucination concerns, human scientists trounce AI agents, a lawyer gets suspended — and why Qin Shi Huang's 2,200-year-old lesson on centralization still holds.

item.title: Issue #6: AI in Your Wallet, 12 Agents Nobody Talks To, and the 2,000-Year Org Chart Problem
item.description: Perplexity moves into financial advisory, 12 agents per enterprise but 50% siloed — and the history of hierarchy from Rome to railroads, what Dorsey gets right about AI dismantling it, and what he's overstating.

item.title: Issue #5: The Next Models Are Coming, Agents Are Scaling — and None of It Matters Until You Rebuild the Process
item.description: GPT-5.5 Spud and Claude Mythos 5 are in the making, AI agents cross from pilot to production at scale, Microsoft Copilot Cowork goes GA — and why AI won't unlock its true power until you redesign the factory around it.

item.title: Issue #4: Shutdowns, Power Struggles, and the Two-Boss Problem
item.description: OpenAI kills Sora ($15M/day burn), Claude fights OpenClaw with remote control, the White House frames AI policy, data centers face energy backlash, token costs exceed human workers — plus: why everyone has two bosses.

item.title: Issue #3: Agents, Jobs, and the Productivity Puzzle
item.description: Jensen Huang declares AI agents the new OS, Karpathy's viral job map sparks backlash, the hard evidence on GenAI productivity tells a complicated story — and why your people are too loyal to you.

item.title: Issue #2: Power, Profit, and Panic — AI's Most Turbulent Week Yet
item.description: GPT-5.4 brings AI to Excel with Wall Street data feeds, Google's Nano Banana 2 hits 650M users, the Pentagon blacklists Anthropic, and the AI consciousness debate erupts.

item.title: Issue #1: The Race Heats Up — GPT-5 Benchmarks, EU AI Act Enforcement Begins
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5 benchmarks leak early, the EU AI Act enters its enforcement phase, and the open-source community rallies around a surprising new contender.

item.title: Special Issue #002: AI Headlines vs. Reality — Capability ≠ Impact
item.description: Block cuts half its workforce "because of AI," a viral scenario predicts a 38% market crash — but how much is signal and how much is narrative? Three takeaways on what the evidence actually says.
